title: Foundations of Phytochemistry: Isolation, Characterization and Bioactive Potential of Medicinal Plants
author: Hossain, Emdad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Medicinal plants continue to serve as a source agent for traditional healing systems and modern drug discovery. Their therapeutic value is attributed to an immense diversity of phytochemicals with complex structures and profound biological activities. The present study provides basic knowledge on the phytochemical constituents of medicinal plants, with emphasis on their isolation, purification, structural elucidation and major biological functions. Analytical perspectives are offered on the major classes of secondary metabolites, methodological advancements in extraction and characterization and the relevance of ethnopharmacological knowledge in guiding scientific inquiry. Potential challenges relating to standardization, chemical variability, reproducibility and bioavailability are critically discussed. Future directions highlight innovations in metabolomics, chemo-informatics and integrative pharmacology that are reshaping the landscape of natural product research. The current work aims to provide a basic understanding of phytochemistry as a foundational pillar of ethnopharmacology and medicinal plant research.
